sample.title: Why Space Companies Are Betting Big on Texas
sample.description:
Already home to SpaceX, Blue Origin and recent moon-landers Firefly Aerospace and Intuitive Machines, Texas is quickly becoming the center of the commercial space universe. Through a newly-formed agency, Texas officials have been doling out grants for space-related research, projects and emerging technology. So what does this mean for the "Lone Star State?" And why has it become such a proving ground for the startups of the future?

SpaceX’s IPO to Mint Millionaires in Poor Texas Border Town: https://bloom.bg/4bsvut7

The company’s spaceport put Brownsville on the map, but locals fear being priced out by newly rich neighbors. 

00:00 How the space sector is changing 
01:08 Firefly Aerospace sees success in Texas 
02:24 Why Texas appeals to the space industry
04:48 SpaceX history in Texas 
06:10 How the state of Texas is now wooing space business
07:08 The changing relationship between industry and NASA
09:45 The SpaceX business model that paved the way

Watch more short documentaries: https://bloom.bg/4wWlMsa

sample.title: Why Gen Z Is Gambling With Its Future
sample.description:
With homeownership out of reach, artificial intelligence cutting job prospects and index funds feeling too slow, many young investors are turning to meme stocks, options and crypto to meet their financial goals.

sample.title: Consumer Prices See Biggest Drop In Six Years In June
sample.description:
The June consumer price index shows that annual inflation is moving closer to the Federal Reserve’s 2% target. Energy put downward pressure on inflation as a lull in the Iran war easing prices for consumers. CNBC’s Alex Harring breaks down what the June CPI reveals about prices.

sample.title: Americans keep getting priced out of fun. Here's how it impacts us at home
sample.description:
"Funflation," the rising cost of concerts, sporting events, and travel, is surging across America. Now, even staying at home and doing activities like gaming and streaming is expensive. That's because of tariffs, a global memory shortage, and fewer customers. CNBC’s Alex Harring has the details.

sample.title: Will The New Housing Bill Lower Housing Costs?
sample.description:
The largest housing bill in decades has just become law after weeks of tense political standoff between President Trump and Congress. The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act focuses on three major reforms. It aims to increase the supply of housing by combining federal incentives for new homes with reforms designed to speed up home construction. The law also seeks to expand access to housing finance while limiting large institutional investors from buying single-family homes. Now comes the real test: can this ambitious legislation actually make housing affordable again?

Credits:
Reporter: Sarah Agostino, Mike Winters
Producer: Juhohn Lee
Editor: Meline Rosales
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Senior Director of Video: Jessica Leibowitz
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: Gary Cohn on Fed Chairman Warsh: I believe he will not touch interest rates
sample.description: Gary Cohn, IBM vice chairman and former Trump NEC director, joined CNBC's "Squawk on the Street" to discuss Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s first semiannual monetary policy testimony to Congress.

sample.title: Guy Spier on living with glioblastoma: "I know that I have to celebrate today"
sample.description:
Glioblastoma is a rare and aggressive brain cancer. There are no truly effective treatments for the disease, with the median survival for a patient lasting just 15 months after diagnosis.

“Somehow I’m going strong,” value investor Guy Spier, who was diagnosed with grade 4 glioblastoma in 2024, tells CNBC’s Becky Quick. “Every day is basically a gift. So I might as well celebrate.”

sample.title: Blanche says he's not a 'yes man' as he's grilled over Trump loyalty
sample.description:
President Donald Trump's attorney general nominee, Todd Blanche, faced tense bipartisan questioning at a U.S. Senate panel. When Republican Senator John Kennedy of Louisiana asked Blanche if he and Trump were friends, Blanche said "I'm his lawyer," before quickly correcting himself: "... was his lawyer. Now, I'm the deputy attorney general."
 
#trump #toddblanche #doj #politics #legal

US attacks Iran for fifth day in a row as Iran says it could intensify its own attacks | BBC News
sample.description:
The US military has launched a fresh wave of of attacks against Iran for the fifth day in a row.
 
US President Donald Trump has warned power plants and bridges could be hit if Tehran doesn’t accept a deal, while Iran says it could intensify its attacks to include other oil and gas exporting routes.
 
With this escalation, what are the prospects for re-establishing peace negotiations?

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

sample.title: How will the social media curfew for UK teens work? #SocialMedia #BBCNews
Trump's new Strait of Hormuz plan | BBC News
sample.description:
We ask the BBC's business editor Simon Jack what President Donald Trump's new strategy in the Strait of Hormuz means for the global economy - and what cards Iran has left to play as it threatens to block the Bab el-Mandeb strait at the mouth of the Red Sea. 

President Trump has tried to exert more control over the Strait of Hormuz as the US-Iran ceasefire crumbles.

This week, Trump announced a 20% toll for all ships traversing the strait. But he quickly changed tack after receiving calls from regional leaders, saying he would instead make "massive" trade and investment deals with Gulf states - ostensibly in exchange for safe passage.

"I don't like the concept of a fee," he later told reporters. "But at the same time, it's not fair that we're protecting this Strait for the entire world."

Trump has also claimed that "oil is flowing like never before, thanks to the awesome power of the United States military," although analysts say the traffic of oil and gas tankers through the Strait of Hormuz is still markedly lower than before the US-Israel war with Iran

Some have warned that global oil reserves could be running low. 

And the White House is under increasing pressure to stabilise the US economy, and lower gas prices, before midterm elections in November. 

New episodes of the Global News Podcast are published twice a day. You can listen here: https://link.mgln.ai/GNP-YT

And you can find more of our YouTube episodes here: https://bbc.in/GlobalNewsPodcast
Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#bbcnews #trump #iran #straitofhormuz #economy #middleeast #war #oil #gulf

00:014 Donald Trump’s new plan in the Strait of Hormuz 
02:10 “Trade and investment” with Gulf countries 
03:13 Will there be an oil crisis?
